project_overview.project_name: Third Additional Financing for Strengthen Ethiopia's Adaptive Safety Net Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: (a) to expand geographic coverage and enhance service delivery of Ethiopia's adaptive rural safety net to improve the well-being of extremely poor and vulnerable households in drought prone communities, and (b) in case of an Eligible Early Response Financing Event, respond promptly and effectively to it
project_overview.beneficiaries: 5.85 million direct beneficiaries across 489 woredas, targeting extremely poor households in rural Ethiopia

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - significant operations in conflict-affected areas including Tigray, Amhara, parts of Oromiya and Afar due to ongoing conflicts

key_components.description: Safety net operations including public works, mother and child package, cash/food transfers to extremely poor households, and complementary livelihood services

key_components.component_name: Improved Shock Responsiveness of the Rural Safety Net
key_components.description: No additional funding allocated under this AF3

key_components.component_name: Systems, Capacity Development, and Program Management Support
key_components.description: Systems development, capacity building, management and administration including ICT equipment, training, grievance mechanisms, and early warning systems

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture through Food Security Coordination Office (FSCO), with Third Party Implementer in areas of high risk of ongoing conflict
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Donor Coordination Team facilitated through Multi-Donor Trust Fund, strong development partner coordination with 13 development partners
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers, use of STEP system, challenges with contract management and delays
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Management Information System (MIS), Independent Verification Agency, quarterly reporting, spot checks, grievance mechanism with toll-free hotline

identified_risks.risk_description: Volatile political situation, increasing insecurity, conflicts in multiple regions affecting program implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Careful mission planning, strategic communication, proper ESF implementation, enhanced transparency and accountability, flexible adaptation to political changes

identified_risks.risk_description: External economic imbalances, very low foreign reserves, exchange rate misalignment, persistently high inflation eroding beneficiary purchasing power
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continued nominal support, benefit level adjustments, focus on livelihood development, improved beneficiary registration through Fayda ID, gradual price liberalization

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential impacts from public works subprojects, social exclusion, sexual exploitation and abuse risks, security risks in conflict areas, limited implementer capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF procedures, capacity building budget allocation, toll-free hotline for grievances, site-specific mitigation measures

identified_risks.risk_description: Delays in AWP&B finalization, insufficient counterpart contribution, TPI complexities, procurement delays, weak contract management
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Timeline adherence for AWP&B, capacity-adjusted project scope, commodity management system review, timely budget release, dedicated procurement staff appointment

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Kebele Appeals Committees established, toll-free hotline being developed, unified case management system
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: 50% women in public works, 45% women in business plans, preferential targeting for women, exemption from public works during pregnancy/childcare, direct transfers to women pilot

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Traffic light system to assess conflict impact on operations, modified procedures for rapid resumption where possible, Third Party Implementer deployment in conflict zones
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia classified as conflict country on World Bank's FY24 Fragility and Conflict-Affected Situations list, multiple concurrent conflicts disrupting implementation, damaged infrastructure worth US$118 million
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Implementation paused in Tigray for over 2 years, ongoing disruptions in Amhara and parts of Oromiya, security risks for project workers and beneficiaries, enhanced monitoring arrangements
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - project addresses impacts of conflict-induced displacement, supports communities affected by internal displacement, provides assistance to conflict-affected populations
